prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(create_reservation_case supplier_instance_a_1)
(select_booking_option supplier_instance_a_1 booking_option_standard)
(allocate_confirmation_channel supplier_instance_a_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(commit_reservation_case supplier_instance_a_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(assign_supplier_agent supplier_instance_a_1 supplier_agent_1)
(claim_supplier_requirement supplier_instance_a_1 supplier_requirement_document confirmation_channel_1)

(release_confirmation_channel supplier_instance_a_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(create_reservation_case supplier_instance_b_1)
(select_booking_option supplier_instance_b_1 booking_option_premium)
(allocate_confirmation_channel supplier_instance_b_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(commit_reservation_case supplier_instance_b_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(release_supplier_agent supplier_instance_a_1 supplier_agent_1)
(assign_supplier_agent supplier_instance_b_1 supplier_agent_1)
(claim_supplier_requirement_b supplier_instance_b_1 supplier_confirmation_record_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(verify_supplier_requirement_b supplier_instance_b_1 supplier_confirmation_record_1 supplier_agent_1)
(allocate_final_booking_record supplier_instance_a_1 supplier_instance_b_1 supplier_requirement_document supplier_confirmation_record_1 final_booking_record_1)
(release_confirmation_channel supplier_instance_b_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(allocate_confirmation_channel supplier_instance_a_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(prepare_booking_record_for_attachment final_booking_record_1 supplier_instance_a_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(release_confirmation_channel supplier_instance_a_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(create_reservation_case consolidated_booking_main)
(select_booking_option consolidated_booking_main booking_option_fallback)
(allocate_confirmation_channel consolidated_booking_main confirmation_channel_1)
(commit_reservation_case consolidated_booking_main confirmation_channel_1)
(attach_supplier_option_detail consolidated_booking_main supplier_option_detail_1)
(attach_supplier_reference_token consolidated_booking_main supplier_reference_token_1)
(attach_document_to_booking_record consolidated_booking_main attachment_document_1 final_booking_record_1)
(prepare_consolidated_for_attachment_processing consolidated_booking_main attachment_document_1 final_booking_record_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(release_supplier_agent supplier_instance_b_1 supplier_agent_1)
(assign_supplier_agent consolidated_booking_main supplier_agent_1)
(bind_supplier_option_detail_and_mark_attachments consolidated_booking_main supplier_option_detail_1 supplier_agent_1 attachment_document_1 final_booking_record_1)
(bind_supplier_reference_and_mark_variant_1 consolidated_booking_main supplier_reference_token_1 attachment_document_1 final_booking_record_1)
(finalize_consolidated_booking consolidated_booking_main)
(attach_confirmation_proof_to_reservation_case consolidated_booking_main confirmation_proof_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(propagate_confirmation_to_consolidated_booking consolidated_booking_main booking_option_fallback confirmation_proof_1)
(release_confirmation_channel consolidated_booking_main confirmation_channel_1)
(create_reservation_case consolidated_booking_secondary)
(select_booking_option consolidated_booking_secondary booking_option_fallback)
(allocate_confirmation_channel consolidated_booking_secondary confirmation_channel_1)
(commit_reservation_case consolidated_booking_secondary confirmation_channel_1)
(detach_supplier_option_detail consolidated_booking_main supplier_option_detail_1)
(detach_supplier_reference_token consolidated_booking_main supplier_reference_token_1)
(attach_supplier_option_detail consolidated_booking_secondary supplier_option_detail_1)
(attach_supplier_reference_token consolidated_booking_secondary supplier_reference_token_1)
(attach_document_to_booking_record consolidated_booking_secondary attachment_document_2 final_booking_record_1)
(prepare_consolidated_for_attachment_processing consolidated_booking_secondary attachment_document_2 final_booking_record_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(release_supplier_agent consolidated_booking_main supplier_agent_1)
(assign_supplier_agent consolidated_booking_secondary supplier_agent_1)
(bind_supplier_option_detail_and_mark_attachments consolidated_booking_secondary supplier_option_detail_1 supplier_agent_1 attachment_document_2 final_booking_record_1)
(bind_supplier_reference_and_mark_variant_1 consolidated_booking_secondary supplier_reference_token_1 attachment_document_2 final_booking_record_1)
(finalize_consolidated_booking consolidated_booking_secondary)
(attach_confirmation_proof_to_reservation_case consolidated_booking_secondary confirmation_proof_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(propagate_confirmation_to_consolidated_booking consolidated_booking_secondary booking_option_fallback confirmation_proof_1)
(release_confirmation_channel consolidated_booking_secondary confirmation_channel_1)
(finalize_supplier_a_with_booking_record supplier_instance_a_1 final_booking_record_1)
(finalize_supplier_b_with_booking_record supplier_instance_b_1 final_booking_record_1)
(allocate_confirmation_channel supplier_instance_a_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(attach_confirmation_proof_to_reservation_case supplier_instance_a_1 confirmation_proof_1 confirmation_channel_1)

(release_confirmation_channel supplier_instance_a_1 confirmation_channel_1)
(allocate_confirmation_channel supplier_instance_b_1 confirmation_channel_1)

(propagate_confirmation_to_supplier_instance_b supplier_instance_b_1 booking_option_premium confirmation_proof_1)
```

message.reasoning:
The problem is complex, but I need to find a plan to achieve the goals: confirmation_propagated for supplier_instance_a_1, supplier_instance_b_1, consolidated_booking_main, and consolidated_booking_secondary.


## Formulating a Strategy  
- The problem involves confirming goals for multiple instances: supplier_instance_a_1, supplier_instance_b_1, consolidated_booking_main, and consolidated_booking_secondary.  
- Need to develop a plan that addresses each instance systematically to ensure all goals are met.  
- Considering how to propagate confirmation across related components for consistency.  
- Exploring dependencies between instances to prioritize the order of confirmation.

## Examining Shared Resources  
- Multiple instances (supplier_instance_a_1, supplier_instance_b_1, etc.) share limited resources like confirmation_channel_1 and supplier_agent_1.  
- This limitation requires sequencing actions like allocating and releasing resources, such as using release_confirmation_channel.  

## Investigating Reservation Processes  
- No cases initially have reserved options, so each must go through create_reservation_case, select_booking_option, and commit_reservation_case.  
- Confirmation propagation requires finalizing each case, attaching proof, and ensuring reservation_option_reserved, which isn't initially set.  

## Analyzing Supplier Instance Paths  
- For supplier_instance_a_1, paths include claiming and verifying supplier requirements, requiring agent assignment and document attachment.  
- For supplier_instance_b_1, confirmation is already linked, simplifying the process compared to supplier_instance_a_1.  

## Evaluating Finalization Steps  
- Finalizing supplier confirmation involves actions like finalize_supplier_confirmation_with_auxiliary_resource, setting confirmation_received and making auxiliary resources available.  
- Different paths exist for achieving confirmation_propagated, depending on whether requirements or confirmations are involved.

## Evaluating Finalization Steps  
- Issuing final_booking_record requires supplier_instance_a_1 and supplier_instance_b_1 to be ready, with requirements verified for a and confirmations verified for b, depending on the variant.  
- Variants like standard, variant_a, and variant_b set flags (e.g., flag_missing_a) based on whether confirmation_received is missing for a or b.  

## Assessing Resource Constraints  
- With only one final_booking_record available and no release action, it can be allocated only once, posing a challenge for four cases.  
- Three booking options exist, but sequential processing allows reusing options after propagation, as each case reserves an option until finalized.  

## Exploring Record Preparation  
- Preparing booking records for attachment requires allocation, readiness, and channel allocation, setting them ready_for_att.  
- Attaching documents to consolidated records binds the proof, consuming availability, with effects like bound_to_record and not_available.  

## Investigating Consolidation Links  
- Consolidated cases are pre-linked to final_record (e.g., consolidated_linked_final_record true initially for main and secondary to final_1).  
- Finalizing supplier instances with the booking record (finalize_supplier_a_with_booking_record) requires readiness and allocation, setting finalized status.

## Examining Supplier Readiness  
- To make supplier_instance_a_1 ready, options include claiming requirements, verifying with an agent, or allocating auxiliary resources.  
- Claiming requirements sets verified status, while verifying with an agent makes it ready and confirms receipt, but doesn't allocate resources.  

## Assessing Auxiliary Allocation  
- Allocating auxiliary resources to supplier_instance_a_1 sets readiness and binds resources, but may not verify requirements initially.  
- Finalizing confirmation with auxiliary resources ensures verified status and releases auxiliary availability, simplifying subsequent steps.  

## Comparing Process Paths  
- For supplier_instance_b_1, readiness and confirmation follow similar paths, with confirmation already linked in initial setup.  
- Using agent-based verification without auxiliaries could minimize resource constraints, given limited availability of both agents and auxiliaries.

## Analyzing Finalization Process  
- Finalizing supplier instances (a1 and b1) requires readiness, confirmation receipt, and final record allocation, setting finalized status.  
- The process doesn't explicitly require a committed state, as readiness already implies commitment.  

## Checking Proof Attachment  
- Attaching confirmation proof to finalized cases requires an allocated channel, available proof, and sets propagation requirements.  
- Proof is released after propagation, allowing reuse across cases, with options needing reservation before selection.  

## Evaluating Option Allocation  
- With only three booking options for four cases, sequential processing is necessary to reuse options after propagation.  
- Processing requires selecting options for a1, b1, and one consolidated case first, leaving the fourth case (e.g., secondary) pending until an option is freed.  

## Exploring Process Sequencing  
- To finalize consolidated cases, both a1 and b1 must be ready, requiring final record allocation after their commitment.  
- This creates a dependency: consolidated cases need options selected, but processing one completely (including propagation) frees options for the next case.

## Managing Resource Reuse  
- Detaching supplier option detail and reference token from the main consolidated booking makes them available for the secondary case.  
- Attaching these resources to consolidated_booking_secondary enables further processing, ensuring readiness for finalization.  

## Finalizing Secondary Case  
- Binding attachments and reference tokens to consolidated_booking_secondary completes the final steps, leading to its finalization.  
- Proof attachment and propagation for the secondary case reuse resources, freeing options like fallback for other cases.  

## Finalizing Supplier Instances  
- Finalizing supplier_instance_a_1 and supplier_instance_b_1 requires only the final booking record, without additional channels or agents.  
- Both instances are committed, ready, and confirmed, allowing straightforward finalization with the allocated record.  

## Handling Channel Allocation  
- Releasing the confirmation channel from consolidated_booking_secondary makes it available for supplier instances.  
- Allocating the channel to supplier_instance_a_1 prepares for proof attachment and propagation in the next steps.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
